opp.name: Hager Group_CLM
opp.next_step: June 29: Demo done by Varun June 8, 2026: New demo meeting for CLM June 29. April 30, 2026: As we are actively working on our AI tool development within Hager and as there is an opportunity to already progress with the legal module/contract revision, would it be possible to get a deeper demo remotely? Feb 20 2026: Caroline followed up February 11, 2026: Onsite 4 hour demo in their German offices Jan 10, 2026: We had a disco call with CPO and Chief of staff, demo to be organized in France mid February Dec 11, 2025: meeting scheduled in January January 9th Dec 08, 2025: Hi Caroline, we are currently organising your presentation of zycus tool with Hager stakeholders and my team beg of January. Since we are late and I speak with my d&i business partner this Monday could you give me an idea of the budget to foresee so I can include it in 2026 plan. I need an envelope for this Monday, apologies for the rush. Thank you 🙏 Nb: of course in case the tool works for us but if I have no budget for sure I cannot foresee anything ! Dec 05, 2025 Met the CPO in Belgium at EY. She reqyested a demo early January which is being arranged as we speak
opp.ais_why:
• Product scope is iRisk, iSource, iSupplier only ‚Äî no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no Merlin Intake, no ML iAnalyze. This is a traditional SRM/Sourcing/Risk deal without any AI-specific module in play. The opp name is 'Hager Group_CLM' but the actual product scope doesn't include iContract or any Merlin variant for CLM.
• Formal Evaluation stage with BD Engaged; last activity May 8, 2026. Zero Avoma meetings returned for this opportunity in the searched window (2024-2026), indicating either off-Avoma conversations or low recorded engagement. There is no captured rep-buyer dialogue to assess AI sentiment from.
• Low confidence: SF Description is null and no Avoma data available, so judgment rests entirely on product mix (no AI SKUs) and stage. Default DOWN at 3/4 applied. Would move UP only if AI products are added to scope or a discovery meeting surfaces buyer AI interest. The absence of Merlin/Merlin Intake/ANA/Agentic AI in product scope is itself a strong negative signal.

items.quote: Buyer requested budget envelope on 8 Dec 2025, noting 'I speak with my d&i business partner this Monday' for 2026 planning approval. This senior business partner (likely CFO or exec sponsor) has never been mapped or engaged.
No pricing discussion, ROI quantification, or commercial proposal logged across 7 months of engagement.
SAP S/4HANA single-instance integration required with D&I team support, but no formal scoping session, timeline, or InfoSec review scheduled.

Only 0 buyer-side contact roles on this opportunity. Need to add Marylene Lombardi (CPO), Coline Zitzmann (VP Procurement), and other engaged stakeholders (Klaus Kirchner, Manon Pudelko, etc.) to the opportunity.

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated operational pain around procurement fragmentation and manual processes but has not quantified impact in cost, cycle time, or savings. On the 9 Jan 2026 discovery call, Marylene Lombardi (CPO) stated 'AGAIR's procurement maturity is currently average with fragmented tools and manual processes, prompting a need for an integrated source-to-pay solution for both direct and indirect spend.' Another buyer noted 'There is a significant challenge with Maverick spending in direct, which is being managed through the categories.' The pain is confirmed and strategic (CPO-owned), but no ROI baseline, savings target, or quantified business case has been developed. On the 11 Feb 2026 on-site demo, the intake manager feature was highlighted as essential for front-end coherence across PR/PO, strategic sourcing, and supplier onboarding, signaling process efficiency as a decision criterion. The lack of quantified metrics means the buyer has no anchored value case to defend budget internally, which is a gap given the CFO/exec sponsor is unmapped. The next move is to run an ROI workshop that quantifies sourcing productivity gains, maverick spend reduction, and supplier risk visibility, tied to Hager's 2026 priorities.

champion.narrative: Coline Zitzmann (VP of Procurement) is the named champion per the MEDDPICC CRM. She attended both the 9 Jan 2026 discovery call and the 11 Feb 2026 on-site demo, and is actively coordinating internal stakeholders. The CPO (Marylene Lombardi) is the senior executive sponsor and has driven the process from the initial EY meeting in Dec 2025 through the recent CLM demo on 29 Jun 2026. The CPO's active engagement (requested the demo, joined the discovery call with her Chief of Staff, attended the 4-hour on-site demo, and initiated the deeper CLM demo request) demonstrates strong, active sponsorship. Coline as VP of Procurement has access to the CPO and is the operational champion coordinating the evaluation. The 8 Dec 2025 budget request was initiated by the buyer (likely Coline or the CPO), showing internal advocacy. No evidence of coaching, internal document sharing, or competitive pushback is visible, so the champion strength is 'developing' rather than 'strong.' The risk is that the champion and CPO are aligned but the CFO/exec sponsor (the 'd&i business partner') is unmapped and unengaged, which could stall budget approval. The next move is to ask Coline or the CPO to facilitate an introduction to the CFO or exec sponsor for a joint ROI and commercial discussion.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been identified across 7 months of engagement (created 28 Nov 2025, now 1 Jul 2026). The Competitors__c field is empty, the MEDDPICC CRM states 'Unknown / Do Nothing,' and no rival is named in any call, Next_Step__c entry, or task. This is either a sole-sourced deal (the buyer skipped an RFP and went direct to Zycus, which is low-risk and favorable) or competitive intelligence is hidden (a competitor exists but is undisclosed, which is high-risk if the rival is ahead). The buyer's behavior suggests sole-sourcing: the CPO requested the demo directly after meeting at an EY event, the buyer initiated the deeper CLM demo request, and the process has progressed through discovery, multi-stakeholder demo, and focused fit validation without any RFP, shortlisting, or vendor comparison language. The 8 Dec 2025 budget request ('in case the tool works for us') implies a single-vendor evaluation. However, the lack of explicit confirmation ('you are the only vendor we are evaluating') is a gap. The wedge is Zycus's integrated S2P platform with the relevant stakeholder and Merlin, positioned as a strategic modernization play for a CPO-led transformation. The next move is to ask the CPO or champion directly: 'Are you evaluating other vendors, or is this a sole-source discussion?' If sole-source, lock it in by accelerating commercials and ROI; if competitive, surface the shortlist and position Zycus's AI and integration depth as the differentiator.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's pain is fragmented procurement tools, manual processes, maverick spend in direct categories, and lack of supplier risk and compliance visibility. On the 9 Jan 2026 discovery call, Marylene Lombardi (CPO) stated 'AGAIR's procurement maturity is currently average with fragmented tools and manual processes, prompting a need for an integrated source-to-pay solution for both direct and indirect spend.' Another buyer noted 'There is a significant challenge with Maverick spending in direct, which is being managed through the categories.' The intake manager was flagged as 'essential for front-end coherence across PR/PO, strategic sourcing and supplier onboarding,' signaling usability and process fragmentation as core pain points. The 30 Apr 2026 request for a deeper CLM demo ('we are actively working on our AI tool development within Hager and there is an opportunity to already progress with the legal module/contract revision') reveals a new pain: legal contract revision bottlenecks and the need for AI-driven contract automation. The pain is strategic (CPO-owned), operational (category managers and sourcing teams), and expanding to legal (CLM). The pain owner is the CPO, with category managers and the legal function as secondary stakeholders.

sources: Avoma call 2026-01-09 (SAP S/4HANA, NDA, D&I team)
paper_process.narrative: The buyer has confirmed technical and integration requirements but no contracting mechanics have been initiated. On the 9 Jan 2026 call, SAP S/4HANA was confirmed as the ERP ('SAP S/4HANA is in place on a single instance, with the internal DNI team designated to support system integration and compatibility'), and an NDA was flagged as a prerequisite for the on-site workshop. The 11 Feb 2026 demo covered supplier risk integrations (D&B, Ecovadis, Trustpair, Cyberwatch) and contract lifecycle automation (AI metadata extraction, WordConnect plugin, SLA tracking, SAP PO/invoice matching), which signal InfoSec, legal review, and ERP integration scoping as required gates. However, no InfoSec review, SAP integration timeline, MSA/SOW discussion, or legal/procurement channel has been scheduled or logged. The MEDDPICC CRM notes the buyer is evaluating sourcing, supplier management, and risk, and 'could expand if the evaluation is successful,' but no formal scoping or contracting path is visible. The buyer's 30 Apr 2026 request for a deeper CLM demo ('opportunity to already progress with the legal module') suggests legal/contracting is a near-term priority. The next move is to propose a joint SAP integration and InfoSec scoping session with Hager's D&I team and IT, tied to a draft SOW and MSA kickoff.

economic_buyer.narrative: The CPO (Marylene Lombardi) is the strongest identified executive sponsor and has been actively engaged since the initial contact in Dec 2025. She requested the demo, joined the 9 Jan 2026 discovery call with her Chief of Staff, and attended the 11 Feb 2026 on-site demo. However, the buyer's 8 Dec 2025 request for a budget envelope noted they would 'speak with my d&i business partner this Monday' for 2026 planning approval, indicating the CPO does not control final budget authority alone. The CFO or a senior exec sponsor (likely the 'd&i business partner' referenced) has not been mapped or engaged. The MEDDPICC CRM (updated 2026-04-08) confirms 'The economic buyer has not been fully confirmed, but the CPO appears to be the strongest executive sponsor currently identified.' The CPO is a confirmed active sponsor with procurement authority, but commercial and budget sign-off require a higher executive (CFO or board-level) who remains unidentified and unengaged. This is a real gap at this stage. The next move is to ask the CPO to introduce the CFO or the 'd&i business partner' for a commercial and ROI discussion, positioning it as a joint planning session to align the business case with Hager's 2026 budget cycle.

sources: Avoma call 2026-01-09 (attendees: CPO, Chief of Staff, EY advisors)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a deliberate, multi-stage process that has escalated in seniority and breadth. The process began with the CPO met at an EY event in Belgium on 5 Dec 2025, followed by a discovery call with the CPO and Chief of Staff on 9 Jan 2026. A multi-stakeholder on-site demo was held on 11 Feb 2026 in Germany (4+ hours, 12 attendees including CPO, category managers, IT, finance, and sourcing leads). The buyer then initiated a deeper CLM-focused demo on 30 Apr 2026, which was delivered on 29 Jun 2026. The escalation pattern shows the deal moving from senior sponsorship (CPO) to cross-functional validation (category managers, IT, finance) to focused fit confirmation (CLM and AI). The 8 Dec 2025 budget request ('I need an envelope for this Monday... I speak with my d&i business partner') indicates the approval chain includes the CPO plus a senior business partner (likely CFO or exec sponsor). The MEDDPICC CRM notes 'There is no confirmed approved budget yet, but there was a direct request from the customer on December 8, 2025 for a budgetary envelope to include in the 2026 plan.' No formal commercials, ROI workshop, or EB approval session has occurred. The next milestone is commercials and ROI validation with the CFO or exec sponsor, followed by SAP integration scoping, InfoSec review, and legal.

sources: Avoma call 2026-02-11, key takeaways and demo content
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has run a structured evaluation with clear, demonstrated criteria. On the 9 Jan 2026 discovery call, the buyer requested 'A comprehensive walkthrough of Zycus's contract, negotiation and risk modules to evaluate their fit.' The on-site demo on 11 Feb 2026 covered sourcing, supplier risk, supplier collaboration, intake management, and SAP integration, with 12 stakeholders including category managers, IT, finance, and the CPO. The intake manager feature was flagged as 'essential for front-end coherence across PR/PO, strategic sourcing and supplier onboarding.' Supplier risk module capabilities (D&B, Ecovadis, geopolitical alerts, AI web-crawl) were demonstrated, and SAP S/4HANA integration was confirmed as a requirement. The 30 Apr 2026 buyer request for a deeper CLM demo ('we are actively working on our AI tool development within Hager and there is an opportunity to progress with the legal module/contract revision') signals CLM and AI-driven contract automation as a new, high-priority criterion. The criteria are: (1) integrated source-to-pay with sourcing, supplier risk, and CLM, (2) intake/front-end coherence, (3) SAP S/4HANA compatibility, (4) AI-driven contract revision and legal automation, (5) supplier risk and ESG visibility. The CLM/AI criterion is the newest and most urgent.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 28 Nov 2025, qualified ~9 Jan 2026 (disco call), 152 days to close. Time in Formal Evaluation: approximately 167 days since creation. The deal has progressed through discovery (9 Jan 2026), multi-stakeholder on-site demo (11 Feb 2026, 4.5 hours, 12 attendees including CPO and category managers), and a recent CLM-focused remote demo (29 Jun 2026, 2 days ago). However, no commercials, pricing, ROI quantification, EB approval, InfoSec review, SAP integration scoping, or contracting have occurred. With 152 days remaining, the realistic path requires: (1) commercials and pricing (2-3 weeks), (2) ROI workshop and CFO/EB sign-off (3-4 weeks), (3) SAP integration scoping with D&I team (2-3 weeks), (4) InfoSec review (2-4 weeks), (5) legal/MSA/SOW (4-6 weeks). Conservative total: 13-20 weeks (91-140 days), which consumes most or all of the remaining runway and leaves no margin. The deal will likely slip to Q1 2027.

CLM demo delivered by Varun on 29 Jun 2026 (2 days ago) (source: Next_Step__c)

Zero commercials, pricing, ROI quantification, or EB approval logged across 7 months
north_star_verdict.headline: The deal is fundamentally healthy with strong buyer engagement and multi-threaded senior stakeholder coverage, but the 30 Nov 2026 close date is not credible.

flags: Quantify the ROI before pricing: Hager articulated clear operational pain (Maverick spend, fragmented tools, manual processes) but no financial baseline or savings target was captured across two demos. On the 29 Jun CLM call, co-build a value model with Lombardi — estimate time saved in contract revision, Maverick-spend reduction %, AI negotiation lift — so commercials land against a number they own, not a vendor claim.

Map the approval chain now: Lombardi requested budget for 2026 plan (8 Dec) but referenced consulting 'd&i business partner' for sign-off. For a 110k investment, confirm on 29 Jun whether she can approve solo or if CFO/exec sponsor must bless it, and if the latter, who and when. Derisk the EB gap before proposal.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is progressing well but lacks commercials, EB approval, and competitive confirmation. The buyer is actively engaged and has validated fit across sourcing, risk, and CLM. The single highest-impact lever is to engage the CFO or exec sponsor for ROI validation and budget sign-off, which unlocks pricing, SAP scoping, and contracting.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer initiated deeper CLM demo on 30 Apr 2026: 'As we are actively working on our AI tool development within Hager and as there is an opportunity to already progress with the legal module/contract revision, would it be possible to get a deeper demo remotely?' — buyer is pulling Zycus into their AI transformation, not benchmarking.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified. The buyer's behavior (CPO-initiated direct engagement, no RFP or shortlisting language, buyer-requested deeper demo) strongly suggests a sole-source evaluation. If confirmed, this is highly favorable; if a hidden competitor exists, it is a high-risk blind spot. The single current threat is 'Do Nothing' (internal build or delayed decision), but the buyer's 30 Apr request to accelerate CLM and the active stakeholder engagement mitigate this.

competitors.name: Unknown / Do Nothing
competitors.quote: No competitor named; buyer pain is fragmented tools and manual processes, suggesting patchwork incumbent or SAP Ariba, but no vendor confirmed
competitors.source: Avoma 9 Jan, 11 Feb; Next Step log; Salesforce Competitors__c unknown
competitors.how_we_win: If sole-sourced: lock champion advocacy, quantify ROI, close fit on 29 Jun CLM demo, move to commercials fast. If competitive round emerges: lead with Merlin negotiation agent + CLM AI + integrated S2P on single codebase as wedge vs point-solution rivals.

competitors.name: Do Nothing
competitors.quote: Buyer requested budget envelope on 8 Dec 2025: 'in case the tool works for us but if I have no budget for sure I cannot foresee anything' — signals budget prioritization risk, but buyer-initiated deeper CLM demo on 30 Apr and active engagement through 29 Jun mitigate.
competitors.how_we_win: Accelerate ROI workshop to quantify maverick spend reduction, sourcing productivity gains, and supplier risk visibility; tie Zycus's integrated S2P + AI-driven CLM to Hager's 2026 transformation plan; engage CFO or exec sponsor to lock budget approval.

items.grounding_quote: A half-day on-site workshop/demo is to be scheduled in France during the first half of February, with prerequisites including an NDA and sample data provision.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong and expanding. The buyer requested a deeper CLM demo on 30 Apr 2026 specifically because 'we are actively working on our AI tool development within Hager and there is an opportunity to already progress with the legal module/contract revision.' The 11 Feb demo showcased Merlin Intake Agent, AI-driven supplier risk (web-crawl for geopolitical alerts), and AI contract metadata extraction. Zycus's AI narrative (Merlin, iContract, agentic automation) is landing as a strategic differentiator tied to Hager's own AI transformation. The score is high.

items.quote: AGAIR's procurement maturity is currently average with fragmented tools and manual processes, prompting a need for an integrated source-to-pay solution for both direct and indirect spend.
items.criterion: Integrated source-to-pay for direct and indirect spend

items.quote: The intake manager feature was highlighted as essential for front-end coherence across PR/PO, strategic sourcing and supplier onboarding.
items.criterion: Intake manager for front-end coherence across PR/PO, sourcing, supplier onboarding

items.quote: Supplier risk module ingests third-party feeds (D&B, Ecovadis, Cyberwatch) plus generative AI web-crawl for geographical and geopolitical alerts.
items.criterion: Supplier risk management (D&B, Ecovadis, geopolitical alerts, AI web-crawl)

items.quote: SAP S/4HANA is in place on a single instance, with the internal DNI team designated to support system integration and compatibility.

As we are actively working on our AI tool development within Hager and as there is an opportunity to already progress with the legal module/contract revision, would it be possible to get a deeper demo remotely?
items.criterion: AI-driven legal contract revision and CLM automation

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on integrated S2P, supplier risk, and intake coherence. The buyer has validated Zycus's sourcing, risk, and supplier collaboration modules through the 11 Feb demo. The new AI-driven CLM and legal contract revision requirement (raised 30 Apr) is a strategic expansion that deepens fit and urgency.

hard.ais_why:
• Product scope is iRisk, iSource, iSupplier only ‚Äî no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no Merlin Intake, no ML iAnalyze. This is a traditional SRM/Sourcing/Risk deal without any AI-specific module in play. The opp name is 'Hager Group_CLM' but the actual product scope doesn't include iContract or any Merlin variant for CLM.
• Formal Evaluation stage with BD Engaged; last activity May 8, 2026. Zero Avoma meetings returned for this opportunity in the searched window (2024-2026), indicating either off-Avoma conversations or low recorded engagement. There is no captured rep-buyer dialogue to assess AI sentiment from.
• Low confidence: SF Description is null and no Avoma data available, so judgment rests entirely on product mix (no AI SKUs) and stage. Default DOWN at 3/4 applied. Would move UP only if AI products are added to scope or a discovery meeting surfaces buyer AI interest. The absence of Merlin/Merlin Intake/ANA/Agentic AI in product scope is itself a strong negative signal.
